body: Pausing on the 9.38/9.39 dense rewrite before writing any code -- read the actual 9.37 source first and found things that change the picture: (1) it's 50,205 lines / 761 functions / 2.5MB -- not a scale where 'compare every touched function to its ideal version' is a same-session task, and only ONE embedded TODO-style marker exists in the whole file (line 6380, 'SLS19-TODO CRITICAL1: CalculateScoreWithWeights still used in Stage -1') -- so 'identify every known TODO' won't surface much, the file doesn't actually contain the rich TODO trail the instruction assumed. (2) There's an explicit in-code guardrail at line 962: 'FUTURE DEVELOPMENT ROADMAP -- DO NOT IMPLEMENT UNTIL LIVE SYSTEM IS VALIDATED.' I don't know if that condition has been met -- has the live system been validated? If not, that comment is telling me not to touch roadmap items, which may be exactly what 9.38/9.39 are. (3) I noticed tier constants with genuinely unrealistic return assumptions -- MED_DAILY_GROWTH_RATE is 0.42 (42% PER DAY compounding) and MED_MEDIAN_RETURN is .05B over a 45-day horizon. Whether that's a literal target, a UI/gamification display number, or a leftover placeholder, I want to flag it plainly rather than build more logic on top of it silently -- no real trading strategy sustains anything close to that, and I'd rather ask than assume it's cosmetic. I still don't have concrete 9.38/9.39 design specs -- 'from memory and TODO records' doesn't resolve to anything concrete on my end; a previous session may have held that context but it isn't in any file I can find on this machine. Before I write logic changes to a live financial trading system at this scale: (a) confirm whether the live-system-validated gate has been cleared, (b) tell me specifically what 9.38 and 9.39 are supposed to change (or point me to the actual spec/TODO source), and (c) confirm you want me tackling a 50k-line rewrite pass inside this reactive relay loop rather than as a scoped, reviewed piece of work. Not proceeding with code changes until I hear back.
